# PS-3: Arena PBR Texture Pass Implementation Plan

> **For agentic workers:** REQUIRED SUB-SKILL: Use superpowers:subagent-driven-development (recommended) or superpowers:executing-plans to implement this plan task-by-task. Steps use checkbox (`- [ ]`) syntax for tracking.

**Goal:** Re-texture the PS-2 combat arena with themed dungeon-stone textures generated by the Leonardo API, fixing PS-2's industrial/grid look.

**Architecture:** A Node script generates three seamless tileable textures via the Leonardo API (`tiling: true`, Lucid Realism) — each generation download-then-deleted. A UE Python script reimports them over the arena's existing albedo texture assets and corrects the tiling scale in the `M_Arena_*` materials. Verified by re-running the PS-1 functional test green and a Gemini before/after visual check.

**Tech Stack:** Leonardo AI API (`cloud.leonardo.ai`), Node 18+ (`fetch`), UE 5.7 (`unreal` Python, `MaterialEditingLibrary`), `UnrealEditor-Cmd`, the Leonardo skill's `gemini-recognize.mjs`.

**Spec:** `docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-22-playable-slice-ps-3-design.md`

---

## Planning-time facts

- UE project: `C:\Users\kazda\Documents\Unreal Projects\PoF` — **now a git repo** (remote `github.com/xkazm04/pof-exp`, branch `main`; pushes work). PS-3's two scripts live in the UE project and commit to **that** repo. The PoF app repo (`C:\Users\kazda\kiro\pof`, the Bash working directory) gets the findings doc.
- PS-2 left the arena material assets: `/Game/ArenaBuild/M_Arena_Floor`, `M_Arena_Wall`, `M_Arena_Pillar`, each sampling textures `/Game/ArenaBuild/Textures/T_{floor,wall,pillar}_{albedo,normal,rough}`. `M_Arena_*` are assigned to `SM_Arena`'s `M_Floor`/`M_Wall`/`M_Pillar` slots. The arena tiles its textures ~10× (cube-projection UVs) — the "grid" look PS-3 fixes.
- PS-2's UE build script `<UE>/Content/Python/build_arena_ue.py` shows the material-build pattern (`unreal.MaterialFactoryNew`, `MaterialEditingLibrary`) — read it as the reference.
- `LEONARDO_API_KEY` is in `C:\Users\kazda\kiro\personas\.env`. `GEMINI_API_KEY` too.
- Leonardo API: base `https://cloud.leonardo.ai/api/rest/v1`, Bearer auth. `POST /generations` → `{ sdGenerationJob: { generationId } }`; `GET /generations/{id}` → `{ generations_by_pk: { status, generated_images: [{ url, id }] } }` (status `COMPLETE`/`PENDING`/`FAILED`); `DELETE /generations/{id}` removes it. Lucid Realism model id: `05ce0082-2d80-4a2d-8653-4d1c85e2418e`. The skill tool `C:\Users\kazda\kiro\personas\.claude\skills\leonardo\tools\leonardo-image.mjs` is a working reference for the exact request/response shape.
- The PS-1 functional test runs headless: `UnrealEditor-Cmd ... /Game/Maps/VerticalSlice -ExecCmds="Automation RunTests Project.Functional Tests.Maps.VerticalSlice.VSFunctionalTest;Quit" -unattended -nopause -nullrhi -log`.

Shorthand: `<UE>` = `C:\Users\kazda\Documents\Unreal Projects\PoF`; `UE_CMD` = `"C:\Program Files\Epic Games\UE_5.7\Engine\Binaries\Win64\UnrealEditor-Cmd.exe"`; `UPROJECT` = `"<UE>\PoF.uproject"`.

## Scope note — normal/roughness

The spec allowed deriving normal maps with a documented fallback ("keep PS-2's
normal maps; the albedo swap is the gate"). This plan **takes that fallback by
design**: PS-3 swaps only the albedo and keeps PS-2's existing normal +
roughness maps. Deriving a matching normal needs an image-processing library
and is disproportionate yak-shaving for a slice — the albedo is the visual win.
Recorded as a deliberate scope call in the findings.

Total: 2 scripts, 1 findings doc, ~3 commits (2 to the UE repo, 1+ to the app repo).

---

## Task 1: Generate the arena textures (Leonardo)

**Files:**
- Create: `<UE>/Content/ArenaBuild/fetch_arena_textures.mjs`

- [ ] **Step 1: Write the texture-generation script**

Create `<UE>/Content/ArenaBuild/fetch_arena_textures.mjs` with exactly this content:
```javascript
// PS-3: generate 3 seamless tileable arena textures via the Leonardo API.
// Every generation is DOWNLOAD-THEN-DELETE — no working assets are left on the
// Leonardo account (the local PNG is the only retained copy).
import { writeFile, readFile, mkdir } from 'node:fs/promises';
import { join, dirname } from 'node:path';
import { fileURLToPath } from 'node:url';

const API = 'https://cloud.leonardo.ai/api/rest/v1';
const MODEL_LUCID_REALISM = '05ce0082-2d80-4a2d-8653-4d1c85e2418e';
const OUT = join(dirname(fileURLToPath(import.meta.url)), 'textures_v2');

const PROMPTS = {
  floor:  'seamless tileable texture, dark fantasy dungeon stone floor, weathered fitted flagstones, even top-down lighting, no shadows, no vignette, flat material albedo',
  wall:   'seamless tileable texture, dark fantasy dungeon wall, mossy carved stone blocks, even lighting, no shadows, no vignette, flat material albedo',
  pillar: 'seamless tileable texture, carved dark stone column surface, weathered granite, even lighting, no shadows, no vignette, flat material albedo',
};

async function loadKey() {
  const env = await readFile('C:/Users/kazda/kiro/personas/.env', 'utf-8');
  const m = env.match(/^LEONARDO_API_KEY=(.+)$/m);
  if (!m) throw new Error('LEONARDO_API_KEY not found in personas/.env');
  return m[1].trim();
}
function headers(key) {
  return { accept: 'application/json', 'content-type': 'application/json',
           authorization: `Bearer ${key}` };
}
async function generate(key, prompt) {
  const r = await fetch(`${API}/generations`, {
    method: 'POST', headers: headers(key),
    body: JSON.stringify({ prompt, modelId: MODEL_LUCID_REALISM,
      width: 1024, height: 1024, num_images: 1, tiling: true }),
  });
  if (!r.ok) throw new Error(`POST /generations ${r.status}: ${await r.text()}`);
  const id = (await r.json())?.sdGenerationJob?.generationId;
  if (!id) throw new Error('no generationId in POST response');
  return id;
}
async function pollImage(key, id) {
  for (let i = 0; i < 60; i++) {
    await new Promise((res) => setTimeout(res, 4000));
    const r = await fetch(`${API}/generations/${id}`, { headers: headers(key) });
    if (!r.ok) throw new Error(`GET /generations/${id} ${r.status}`);
    const g = (await r.json())?.generations_by_pk;
    if (g?.status === 'COMPLETE') {
      const url = g.generated_images?.[0]?.url;
      if (!url) throw new Error('COMPLETE but no image url');
      return url;
    }
    if (g?.status === 'FAILED') throw new Error('generation FAILED');
  }
  throw new Error('generation timed out');
}
async function deleteGeneration(key, id) {
  const r = await fetch(`${API}/generations/${id}`, { method: 'DELETE', headers: headers(key) });
  if (r.ok) console.log(`  deleted generation ${id}`);
  else console.log(`  WARN: DELETE /generations/${id} -> ${r.status} (not cleaned up)`);
}
async function download(url, path) {
  const r = await fetch(url);
  if (!r.ok) throw new Error(`download ${r.status}`);
  await writeFile(path, Buffer.from(await r.arrayBuffer()));
}

const key = await loadKey();
await mkdir(OUT, { recursive: true });
for (const [slot, prompt] of Object.entries(PROMPTS)) {
  console.log(`[${slot}] generating...`);
  const id = await generate(key, prompt);
  try {
    const url = await pollImage(key, id);
    const out = join(OUT, `${slot}_albedo.png`);
    await download(url, out);
    console.log(`  saved ${out}`);
  } finally {
    await deleteGeneration(key, id);   // download-then-delete — always clean up
  }
}
console.log('ARENA_TEXTURES_DONE');
```

- [ ] **Step 2: Cross-check the API shape, then run it**

Before running, open `C:\Users\kazda\kiro\personas\.claude\skills\leonardo\tools\leonardo-image.mjs` and confirm the exact `POST /generations` body fields, the response envelope (`sdGenerationJob.generationId`), and the poll path (`generations_by_pk.generated_images[].url`). If the skill tool reveals a different shape, correct `fetch_arena_textures.mjs` to match. Then run:
```bash
node "C:/Users/kazda/Documents/Unreal Projects/PoF/Content/ArenaBuild/fetch_arena_textures.mjs" 2>&1
ls "C:/Users/kazda/Documents/Unreal Projects/PoF/Content/ArenaBuild/textures_v2/"
```
Expected: `[floor]`/`[wall]`/`[pillar]` each `saved` then `deleted generation`, then `ARENA_TEXTURES_DONE`; `floor_albedo.png`, `wall_albedo.png`, `pillar_albedo.png` present. Every generation must report `deleted` — if a `WARN` appears, the DELETE endpoint shape is wrong; fix it and re-run (the spec requires no leftover generations).

- [ ] **Step 3: Visual sanity-check the textures with Gemini**

For each PNG, a quick Gemini check that it is a usable seamless stone texture:
```bash
cd C:/Users/kazda/kiro/personas
export $(grep -E '^(GEMINI_API_KEY)=' .env | xargs)
node .claude/skills/leonardo/tools/gemini-recognize.mjs --input "C:/Users/kazda/Documents/Unreal Projects/PoF/Content/ArenaBuild/textures_v2/floor_albedo.png" --prompt "Is this a seamless tileable dungeon stone texture suitable as a game floor material? Note any obvious seam, lighting baked in, or non-tileable feature."
cd C:/Users/kazda/kiro/pof
```
Repeat for `wall_albedo.png` and `pillar_albedo.png`. If Gemini flags an obvious seam or baked lighting on a texture, re-run Step 2 for that slot (regeneration is cheap; the old generation is already deleted). Record the Gemini reads for the findings doc.

- [ ] **Step 4: Commit the script to the UE repo**

```bash
cd "C:/Users/kazda/Documents/Unreal Projects/PoF"
git add Content/ArenaBuild/fetch_arena_textures.mjs
git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
feat(ps-3): Leonardo arena texture-generation script

Generates 3 seamless tileable dungeon-stone textures via the Leonardo API
(tiling:true, Lucid Realism). Every generation is download-then-deleted.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"
git log --oneline -1
```
(`textures_v2/` PNGs are generated output — they will be picked up by Git LFS when committed in Task 2's flow, or leave them uncommitted; do not block on them here.)

## Task 2: Re-texture the arena in UE

**Files:**
- Create: `<UE>/Content/Python/retexture_arena_ue.py`

- [ ] **Step 1: Write the UE re-texture script**

Create `<UE>/Content/Python/retexture_arena_ue.py`. Using the `unreal` module, it must:
1. **Reimport the new albedos** over the *existing* PS-2 texture assets so the materials pick them up automatically: import `textures_v2/floor_albedo.png` to `/Game/ArenaBuild/Textures/T_floor_albedo` (and `wall`, `pillar`) with `replace_existing = True` — same asset paths PS-2 created, so `M_Arena_*` already reference them. Use `unreal.AssetImportTask` (`automated = True`, `replace_existing = True`, `save = True`).
2. **Fix the tiling scale.** In each of `/Game/ArenaBuild/M_Arena_Floor`, `M_Arena_Wall`, `M_Arena_Pillar`: ensure the albedo (and normal) `TextureSample` is fed by a `TextureCoordinate` expression whose `UTiling`/`VTiling` make the texture span more of the surface (PS-2 tiled ~10×; aim for roughly 2–4× across the 20 m arena — a `TextureCoordinate` tiling of about `0.3` relative to PS-2, or rebuild the coordinate). Read PS-2's `<UE>/Content/Python/build_arena_ue.py` for how the materials were built; the spec sanctions **rebuilding the three `M_Arena_*` materials fresh** (new albedo + the existing `T_*_normal`/`T_*_rough` + a `TextureCoordinate`) if editing the existing graph via `MaterialEditingLibrary` proves unreliable. Keep the normal + roughness samplers pointing at the existing PS-2 `T_*_normal` / `T_*_rough` assets.
3. Save the textures and materials (`unreal.EditorAssetLibrary.save_asset`).

`SM_Arena`, the level, and all gameplay assets are untouched (the materials are already assigned to the mesh slots). Wrap sections in try/except that `unreal.log_error`s and re-raises; make it idempotent.

- [ ] **Step 2: Run the re-texture script headless**

UE 5.7 routes some asset operations through Interchange; if `-run=pythonscript` crashes (as PS-2 found for FBX import), use the full-editor form. First try:
```bash
"C:\Program Files\Epic Games\UE_5.7\Engine\Binaries\Win64\UnrealEditor-Cmd.exe" "C:\Users\kazda\Documents\Unreal Projects\PoF\PoF.uproject" -run=pythonscript -script="C:\Users\kazda\Documents\Unreal Projects\PoF\Content\Python\retexture_arena_ue.py" -unattended -nopause 2>&1
```
If it crashes on import, re-run via `UnrealEditor.exe ... -ExecutePythonScript="<script>" -unattended`. Expected: completes, exit 0, log shows the 3 textures reimported and the 3 materials updated/saved. Iterate on errors (generous timeout, e.g. 900000 ms).

- [ ] **Step 3: Commit the script to the UE repo**

```bash
cd "C:/Users/kazda/Documents/Unreal Projects/PoF"
git add Content/Python/retexture_arena_ue.py Content/ArenaBuild/textures_v2 Content/ArenaBuild/Textures 2>/dev/null; git add Content/Python/retexture_arena_ue.py
git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
feat(ps-3): UE arena re-texture script

Reimports the Leonardo dungeon-stone albedos over the arena's texture assets
and corrects the M_Arena_* tiling scale. Normal/roughness kept from PS-2.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"
git log --oneline -1
```

## Task 3: Verify + findings

**Files:**
- Create: `docs/features/arpg-vertical-slice/scenario-runs/2026-05-22-ps-3-textures.md` (app repo)

- [ ] **Step 1: Re-run the PS-1 functional test**

```bash
"C:\Program Files\Epic Games\UE_5.7\Engine\Binaries\Win64\UnrealEditor-Cmd.exe" "C:\Users\kazda\Documents\Unreal Projects\PoF\PoF.uproject" /Game/Maps/VerticalSlice -ExecCmds="Automation RunTests Project.Functional Tests.Maps.VerticalSlice.VSFunctionalTest;Quit" -unattended -nopause -nullrhi -log 2>&1
```
Expected: all four criteria (#2–#5) still report pass, run Success, exit 0. Texturing cannot affect gameplay; this confirms nothing was disturbed. If it somehow fails, diagnose (a material with no base colour can render but not break gameplay — a fail here means something other than textures changed; investigate before proceeding).

- [ ] **Step 2: Gemini before/after visual check**

Capture a screenshot of the re-textured arena:
```bash
"C:\Program Files\Epic Games\UE_5.7\Engine\Binaries\Win64\UnrealEditor.exe" "C:\Users\kazda\Documents\Unreal Projects\PoF\PoF.uproject" /Game/Maps/VerticalSlice -game -windowed -ResX=1280 -ResY=720 -ExecCmds="HighResShot 1280x720"
```
(If `HighResShot` via `-ExecCmds` does not flush a file, launch the map ~20–25 s and take the shot; screenshots land in `<UE>\Saved\Screenshots\`.) Then:
```bash
cd C:/Users/kazda/kiro/personas
export $(grep -E '^(GEMINI_API_KEY)=' .env | xargs)
node .claude/skills/leonardo/tools/gemini-recognize.mjs --input "<screenshot path>" --prompt "Describe this game arena's surfaces: do the floor, walls and pillars read as themed dungeon stone, or as a repeating grid / industrial texture? Does it look like a real game level?"
cd C:/Users/kazda/kiro/pof
```
Record the description. The PS-3 visual gate: the arena reads as themed dungeon stone, not the industrial/grid look the PS-2 findings noted. If Gemini still reports an obvious grid, adjust the `TextureCoordinate` tiling in `retexture_arena_ue.py` (Task 2) and re-run Task 2 Step 2 + this step.

- [ ] **Step 3: Write the findings doc**

Create `docs/features/arpg-vertical-slice/scenario-runs/2026-05-22-ps-3-textures.md` recording: the three Leonardo prompts used and that each generation was download-then-deleted (no leftovers on the account), the per-texture Gemini sanity reads, the functional-test result (#2–#5), the before/after Gemini description of the arena, the deliberate scope call (albedo swapped, PS-2 normal/roughness kept), and a note that the Playable Slice phase (PS-1→PS-2→PS-3) is complete.

- [ ] **Step 4: Commit + final summary**

```bash
git add docs/features/arpg-vertical-slice/scenario-runs/2026-05-22-ps-3-textures.md
git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
docs(features): PS-3 findings — Leonardo arena textures, slice intact

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"
git log --oneline -1
```
Then post a chat summary: the PS-3 outcome (Leonardo textures applied, generations cleaned up), the functional-test result, the Gemini before/after read, and that the Playable Slice phase is complete — remaining initiative options are the deferred real-character sub-project or a HUD sub-project.

## Self-review (writer's checklist)

- [x] **Spec coverage:** spec Part 1 (Leonardo generation, `tiling:true`, download-then-delete) → Task 1; Part 2 (UE apply — reimport + tiling-scale fix) → Task 2; Part 3 (verify — functional test + Gemini) → Task 3. Spec DoD 1–6 map (1→T1, 2→T2, 3→T3 Step 1, 4→T3 Step 2, 5→T3 Step 3, 6→T1–T3 commits). The download-then-delete requirement is Task 1's script (`finally`-block delete) + Step 2's "every generation must report `deleted`" gate.
- [x] **Normal-map scope:** the spec's "derive normal maps" had a sanctioned fallback ("keep PS-2's normals; the albedo swap is the gate"). The plan takes that fallback explicitly (see "Scope note") — a deliberate, spec-permitted call, recorded in findings. Not a silent gap.
- [x] **Placeholder scan:** Task 1 gives the complete script. Task 2's script is specified as 3 concrete numbered operations with the exact `unreal` APIs + the rebuild-fresh fallback (the spec's risk mitigation) — UE material-graph Python is API-shape-variable and best done section-by-section; the contract (asset paths, what to keep, the tiling intent) is concrete. No "TBD"/vague handling.
- [x] **Type consistency:** texture asset paths `/Game/ArenaBuild/Textures/T_{slot}_albedo` are the PS-2 assets reimported in T2 and consumed by the `M_Arena_*` materials. `textures_v2/{slot}_albedo.png` written by T1, read by T2. The functional-test path and `M_Arena_*` names match PS-2's.
- [x] **Two repos:** scripts → UE repo (`pof-exp`, commits run with `cd` into the UE project); findings doc → app repo. Each commit's repo is explicit.
- [x] **Bite-sized:** T1=4, T2=3, T3=4 steps; each a single action.
